LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO THE OCT-01-2025 HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS PORTCO INC., AND ARTHUR HOPPE'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE UNOPPOSED MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT PREVIOUSLY SOUGHT THIS RELIEF FOR PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO RESPOND TO DISCOVERY. THE COURT GAVE PLAINTIFF ADDITIONAL OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH HIS DISCOVERY OBLIGATIONS, BUT THE CASE IS NOW ON THE EVE OF TRIAL AND LESSER SANCTIONS HAVE NOT PROVEN EFFECTIVE. DEFENDANT WILL BE PREJUDICED IF FORCED TO GO TO TRIAL WITHOUT DISCOVERY. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-01-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS PORTCO INC., AND ARTHUR HOPPE's Motion For Terminating Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The unopposed motion for terminating sanctions is granted. Defendant previously sought this relief for plaintiff's failure to respond to discovery. The court gave plaintiff additional opportunity to comply with his discovery obligations, but the case is now on the eve of trial and lesser sanctions have not proven effective. Defendant will be prejudiced if forced to go to trial without discovery.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Jul-16-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD AS TO PLAINTIFF MANUEL PEREZ.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion for withdrawal of attorney of record is granted. Counsel is ordered to serve on Manuel Perez a copy of the outstanding discovery requests with the court's order granting withdrawal.

Counsel is to prepare and submit a proposed order on the mandatory judicial council form (MC-053).

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-09-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. Harout Messrelian, Esq. of Messrelian Law Inc. (tel: 818-484-6531) for the Plaintiff.
Atty. Jerome Schreibstein, Esq. of Law Office of Jerome Schreibstein (tel: 415-298-1030) for the Defendants.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS PORTCO INC., AND ARTHUR HOPPE's Motion For Terminating Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants Portco Inc. et al. move for terminating sanctions against plaintiff Manuel Quintero Perez. The motion is denied as to terminating sanctions but the court orders alternative sanctions and compliance with outstanding discovery as set out below.

Plaintiff is out of contact with his counsel; counsel served boilerplate objections to Portco's Form and Special Interrogatories and Requests for Production of Documents on February 14, 2025. Portco now seeks terminating sanctions. Generally terminating sanctions are a last resort; this is the first discovery motion in the case. It is possible that Plaintiff will resume contact with counsel and the parties are not so close to trial that a lesser sanction will force Portco to go to trial without necessary discovery.

The court orders Plaintiff to amend his responses to Portco's Form and Special Interrogatories and Requests for Production of Documents within 30 days of entry of this order. The court further orders Plaintiff to pay $500 in discovery sanctions to Portco within 30 days.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
